accounts.name: admin
docs: Registry admin — authorized via the registry admin set.

accounts.name: registry
docs: Singleton registry. `mut` to decrement `market_count` — the
witness `close_registry` later checks is zero.

accounts.name: market
docs: The market being closed. `mut` so its lamports can be drained and
its discriminator scrubbed by `Slab::close`. Taken bare, matching
every other handler; the treasury `address` constraints below
reference this market's stored treasury addresses, so a
mismatched market is rejected before the close.

accounts.name: base_treasury
docs: The base treasury — must already be closed (zero lamports).
CHECK: pinned to `market.base_treasury` and required closed.

accounts.name: quote_treasury
docs: The quote treasury — must already be closed (zero lamports).
CHECK: pinned to `market.quote_treasury` and required closed.

accounts.name: rent_recipient
docs: Receives the market account's rent lamports on close.
CHECK: rent destination only.

accounts.name: admin
docs: Registry admin — authorized via the registry admin set.

accounts.name: registry
docs: Singleton registry, read for the admin-membership check.

accounts.name: market
docs: Market owning the treasury. Read-only — closing a treasury does
not mutate market state (the market is closed separately, after
both treasuries are gone). The market PDA still signs the
`CloseAccount` CPI via its `(base_mint, quote_mint)` seeds,
recovered from `market.bump`. Taken bare (no `seeds` constraint),
matching every other handler: the `associated_token::authority =
market` constraint on `treasury` already binds the ATA to this
market, and the CPI signature fails if a non-matching market is
passed.

accounts.name: mint
docs: One of the market's two leg mints. The ATA constraint below binds
`treasury` to the canonical `(market, mint)` ATA; the handler
additionally rejects any mint that isn't one of the market legs.

accounts.name: token_program
docs: Token program owning `mint`.

accounts.name: treasury
docs: The treasury ATA to close. The ATA constraint pins it to
`ata(market, mint, token_program)`, so a non-canonical account is
rejected before the handler runs. Must be drained to zero.

accounts.name: rent_recipient
docs: Receives the treasury's rent lamports on close.
CHECK: rent destination only; no constraints required — the admin
chooses where reclaimed rent lands.

accounts.name: admin
docs: The last remaining registry admin — the only signer this accepts,
and the one whose authority the close is performed under.

accounts.name: registry
docs: The registry being closed. `mut` so its lamports can be drained
and its discriminator scrubbed by `Slab::close`.

accounts.name: rent_recipient
docs: Receives the registry account's rent lamports on close.
CHECK: rent destination only.

accounts.name: admin
docs: Registry admin — authorized via the registry admin set.

accounts.name: registry
docs: Singleton registry. Read-only — the registry account itself is
closed separately by `close_registry`. The registry PDA signs the
`CloseAccount` CPI via its `[b"registry"]` seed.

accounts.name: fee_mint
docs: Mint of the fee vault being closed. The program may have charged
fees in more than one mint over its life; the admin closes each
fee ATA in turn (the historical set is tracked off-chain).

accounts.name: token_program
docs: Token program owning `fee_mint`.

accounts.name: fee_vault
docs: The fee ATA to close — pinned to `ata(registry, fee_mint,
token_program)` by the constraint. Must be drained to zero (via
the existing admin fee sweep) first.

accounts.name: rent_recipient
docs: Receives the fee vault's rent lamports on close.
CHECK: rent destination only.

accounts.name: signer
docs: Must equal `vault.leader` — verified in-handler.

accounts.name: market
docs: Market holding the target vault. `mut` because the active /
tombstone list heads and `active_count` change.

accounts.name: event_authority
docs: CHECK: Only the event authority can invoke self-CPI

accounts.name: program
docs: CHECK: Kept for v1-compatible account ordering and IDL shape

accounts.name: payer
docs: Funds rent for the market PDA and the two treasury ATAs, and
signs the fee transfer unless waived for an admin signer.

accounts.name: registry
docs: Singleton registry. Mutated to bump `market_count`, and read
for the fee config, admin set, and stamped defaults.

accounts.name: base_mint
docs: Base leg mint. `InterfaceAccount<Mint>` accepts SPL Token and
Token-2022 by relaxing the owner check; the deserialization
rejects non-mint payloads (token accounts, multisigs, etc.).

accounts.name: quote_mint
docs: Quote leg mint. Same deal as `base_mint`.

accounts.name: base_token_program
docs: SPL Token or Token-2022 — whichever owns `base_mint`.
`Interface<TokenInterface>` rejects any other address up front
with `IncorrectProgramId`; an actual `(mint, token_program)`
owner mismatch surfaces later when the ATA program CPIs through
to it.

accounts.name: quote_token_program
docs: Same for the quote leg.

accounts.name: market
docs: Market PDA seeded by `(base_mint, quote_mint)`. The slab tail
holds the vault sectors; we open with zero capacity here and
let `CreateVault` grow it. The `init` constraint enforces
single-shot creation — a second `create_market` against the
same pair is rejected by the runtime before our handler runs.

accounts.name: base_treasury
docs: Pooled base inventory for every vault on this market. The ATA
program is the only path that creates it, and its
`InitializeAccount3` CPI to the token program is what enforces
`base_mint` is a real mint under the supplied token program.

accounts.name: quote_treasury
docs: Pooled quote inventory. See `base_treasury`.

accounts.name: fee_mint
docs: Mint the create-market fee is charged in. The `address` constraint
binds it to whatever was stamped onto the registry at `init`,
so a wrong mint here yields `ConstraintAddress` before the
handler runs.

accounts.name: fee_token_program
docs: Token program that owns `fee_mint`. Pinned to the value the
registry stamped at `init`, so a caller-supplied mismatch is
rejected up front. The `Interface<TokenInterface>` bound is
belt-and-braces — the address check already implies it.

accounts.name: payer_fee_source
docs: Caller's source ATA holding the fee mint. Only read on the
non-admin path — admins skip the transfer entirely, so the
unchecked passthrough is safe: any account here is ignored on
the admin branch.

accounts.name: registry_fee_treasury
docs: Registry's fee vault — created at `init` time. The
`associated_token::*` constraints re-validate it against
`(registry, fee_mint, fee_token_program)` so a non-canonical
destination is rejected by `ConstraintAddress`.

accounts.name: payer
docs: Pays sector-rent top-up (if the slab realloc grows the
account) and the create-vault fee (unless waived for an admin).
Becomes the vault's `leader` unless an admin supplied a
distinct `leader_override` — see the handler.

accounts.name: registry
docs: Singleton registry — read for admin check + the
`max_vaults_per_market` cap.

accounts.name: market
docs: Market the vault lives on. `mut` because the slab tail grows
(or pops from the free list) and `active_count` increments.

accounts.name: fee_mint
docs: Mint the create-vault fee is paid in. Pinned to the value
`create_market` stamped into `market.fee_config.mint`.

accounts.name: fee_token_program
docs: Token program owning `fee_mint`. Pinned to the value stamped at
market creation.

accounts.name: payer_fee_source
docs: Caller's source ATA for the fee mint. Only read on the
non-admin path; admin path skips the transfer entirely.

accounts.name: registry_fee_treasury
docs: Registry's fee ATA. ATA constraint validates
`(registry, fee_mint, fee_token_program)` so a wrong destination
is rejected before the transfer CPI runs.

accounts.name: event_authority
docs: CHECK: Only the event authority can invoke self-CPI

accounts.name: program
docs: CHECK: Kept for v1-compatible account ordering and IDL shape

accounts.name: signer
docs: The outside depositor (creates / tops off `vault_depositor`).
The leader is rejected here — their deposits go through
`deposit_leader`, which carries no `VaultDepositor` PDA.

accounts.name: market
docs: Market the vault lives on. Mut for share + inventory writes.

accounts.name: vault_depositor
docs: `init_if_needed` so a first-time depositor pays rent inline
and a top-off depositor sees the existing PDA. Seeds bind to
`(market, sector_idx, signer)` so signer ownership is
implicit — there is no separate `authority` field to
reassign. The handler closes this PDA on zero-share exit
(`withdraw`) so the depositor's rent comes back.

accounts.name: base_mint
docs: Base mint — pinned to `market.base_mint`.

accounts.name: quote_mint
docs: Quote mint — pinned to `market.quote_mint`.

accounts.name: signer_base_ata
docs: Signer's source ATA for base. Anchor's ATA constraint verifies
`(signer, base_mint, base_token_program)`.

accounts.name: signer_quote_ata
docs: Signer's source ATA for quote.

accounts.name: market_base_treasury
docs: Market base treasury — must match `market.base_treasury`.

accounts.name: market_quote_treasury
docs: Market quote treasury — must match `market.quote_treasury`.

accounts.name: event_authority
docs: CHECK: Only the event authority can invoke self-CPI

accounts.name: program
docs: CHECK: Kept for v1-compatible account ordering and IDL shape

accounts.name: signer
docs: Must equal `vault.leader` — verified in-handler.

accounts.name: market
docs: Market the vault lives on.

accounts.name: event_authority
docs: CHECK: Only the event authority can invoke self-CPI

accounts.name: program
docs: CHECK: Kept for v1-compatible account ordering and IDL shape

accounts.name: admin
docs: Registry admin. Funds the `owner` payout ATAs when
`init_if_needed` has to allocate them.

accounts.name: registry
docs: Singleton registry, read for the admin-membership check.

accounts.name: market
docs: Market the vault lives on.

accounts.name: owner
docs: The depositor whose position is being drained. Not a signer —
that is the whole point of the admin force path. Used as the PDA
seed and the payout-ATA authority so funds and rent both flow to
the depositor, never the admin.
`mut` because the closed `VaultDepositor` PDA refunds its rent
here — the runtime rejects crediting lamports to a readonly account.
CHECK: constrained by the `vault_depositor` PDA seeds (which bind
this key) and the `associated_token::authority` constraints on
the payout ATAs below.

accounts.name: vault_depositor
docs: The depositor's basis PDA. Closed to `owner` on full drain.

accounts.name: owner_base_ata
docs: Depositor's base payout ATA — created (admin-funded) if absent.

accounts.name: owner_quote_ata
docs: Depositor's quote payout ATA — created (admin-funded) if absent.

accounts.name: event_authority
docs: CHECK: Only the event authority can invoke self-CPI

accounts.name: program
docs: CHECK: Kept for v1-compatible account ordering and IDL shape

accounts.name: admin
docs: Registry admin. Funds the leader payout ATAs when `init_if_needed`
has to allocate them.

accounts.name: registry
docs: Singleton registry, read for the admin-membership check.

accounts.name: market
docs: Market the vault lives on.

accounts.name: leader
docs: The vault's leader — the payout target. Not a signer. Verified
in-handler against `vault.leader`.
CHECK: matched against `vault.leader` in the handler and bound as
the `associated_token::authority` of the payout ATAs below.

accounts.name: leader_base_ata
docs: Leader's base payout ATA — created (admin-funded) if absent.

accounts.name: leader_quote_ata
docs: Leader's quote payout ATA — created (admin-funded) if absent.

accounts.name: event_authority
docs: CHECK: Only the event authority can invoke self-CPI

accounts.name: program
docs: CHECK: Kept for v1-compatible account ordering and IDL shape

accounts.name: admin
docs: Registry admin — the only signer this lever accepts.

accounts.name: registry
docs: Singleton registry, read for the admin-membership check.

accounts.name: market
docs: Market holding the target vault. `mut` for the `frozen` write.

accounts.name: event_authority
docs: CHECK: Only the event authority can invoke self-CPI

accounts.name: program
docs: CHECK: Kept for v1-compatible account ordering and IDL shape

accounts.name: program_data
docs: SAFETY: the program's ProgramData account is owned by the BPF
upgradeable loader, not this program, so it cannot be a typed
`Account<T>`. `verify_upgrade_authority` re-derives the
canonical `ProgramData` PDA and only reads its header to
authenticate the upgrade authority — no data is written and
no other invariant is assumed. A declarative
`seeds = [crate::ID.as_ref()], seeds::program = …` would emit
an opaque `{"kind":"expr"}` seed that anchor v2's IDL spec
can't deserialize today; the manual check sidesteps that.

accounts.name: fee_mint
docs: The mint to charge fees in. `InterfaceAccount<Mint>` validates
SPL Token / Token-2022 ownership and that the data unpacks as a
`Mint` — so no separate length / discriminator check is needed.

accounts.name: fee_vault
docs: Registry-owned fee vault for the per-`CreateVault` charge. Created
here by CPI to the ATA program; its address is the canonical
ATA over `(registry, token_program, fee_mint)`, and the ATA
program rejects any `(mint, token_program)` pair whose owners
disagree — a second backstop after `InterfaceAccount<Mint>`.

accounts.name: token_program
docs: Token program owning `fee_mint` — SPL Token or Token-2022.
`Interface<TokenInterface>` rejects any other address up front
(`IncorrectProgramId`); the ATA seeds bake this in too, so a
caller-supplied mismatch against `fee_mint`'s owner yields a
non-canonical ATA and a separate `InvalidSeeds` rejection.

accounts.name: signer
docs: The vault's leader — the only signer this setter accepts.

accounts.name: market
docs: Market holding the target vault.

accounts.name: signer
docs: Quote authority — same gate as `set_reference_price`.

accounts.name: market
docs: Market account holding the target vault.

accounts.name: admin
docs: Registry admin — authorized via the registry admin set.

accounts.name: registry
docs: Singleton registry, read for the admin-membership check.

accounts.name: market
docs: Market holding the target vault.

accounts.name: signer
docs: Quote authority — the only signer the spec accepts here. Set at
`CreateVault` (defaults to the leader).

accounts.name: market
docs: Market account holding the target vault.

accounts.name: clock
docs: Read for the current slot. The leader-supplied `quote_slot` is
validated against this (no future-dating, capped backdate).

accounts.name: taker
docs: Taker.

accounts.name: market
docs: Market the target vault lives on.

accounts.name: event_authority
docs: CHECK: Only the event authority can invoke self-CPI

accounts.name: program
docs: CHECK: Kept for v1-compatible account ordering and IDL shape

accounts.name: signer
docs: Either the vault's leader (burns `leader_shares`) or an outside
depositor (burns the PDA's `shares`). PDA seeds bind the
outside path to this signer.

accounts.name: market
docs: Market the vault lives on.

accounts.name: vault_depositor
docs: Outside depositor's PDA. Mut so we can decrement `shares` and
stamp realized PnL. The handler calls
[`anchor_lang_v2::AnchorAccount::close`] explicitly when
post-burn `shares == 0`, refunding the rent to the signer
and decrementing `outstanding_vault_depositors` — a manual
close keeps the conditional rent-refund logic in one place
instead of relying on Anchor's unconditional `close = signer`
attribute.

accounts.name: event_authority
docs: CHECK: Only the event authority can invoke self-CPI

accounts.name: program
docs: CHECK: Kept for v1-compatible account ordering and IDL shape

accounts.name: signer
docs: Must equal `vault.leader` — verified in-handler.

accounts.name: market
docs: Market the vault lives on.

accounts.name: event_authority
docs: CHECK: Only the event authority can invoke self-CPI

accounts.name: program
docs: CHECK: Kept for v1-compatible account ordering and IDL shape

errors.name: InvalidProgramDataAddress
errors.msg: program_data account is not the canonical PDA for this program

errors.name: InvalidProgramData
errors.msg: program_data account contents could not be decoded

errors.name: InvalidUpgradeAuthority
errors.msg: Init must be signed by the program's upgrade authority

errors.name: AdminSetFull
errors.msg: the registry admin set has no room for another admin

errors.name: AlreadyAdmin
errors.msg: the named pubkey is already a registry admin

errors.name: Unauthorized
errors.msg: signer is not a registry admin

errors.name: AdminNotFound
errors.msg: the named pubkey is not a registry admin

errors.name: CannotRemoveLastAdmin
errors.msg: cannot remove the last remaining registry admin

errors.name: InvalidFeeMint
errors.msg: fee mint does not match the registry's configured fee mint

errors.name: DuplicateBaseQuoteMint
errors.msg: base and quote mints must differ

errors.name: InvalidSectorIndex
errors.msg: supplied sector index is out of range

errors.name: CorruptVaultList
errors.msg: vault list pointers are inconsistent with the list head

errors.name: MarketCountOverflow
errors.msg: registry market_count cannot exceed u32::MAX

errors.name: VaultCapExceeded
errors.msg: market vault cap (registry.max_vaults_per_market) is full

errors.name: InvalidPerfFeeRate
errors.msg: perf_fee_rate exceeds 1_000_000 ppm (100%)

errors.name: LeaderOverrideNotAllowed
errors.msg: non-admin caller cannot open a vault on someone else's behalf

errors.name: VaultEmpty
errors.msg: supplied vault sector is not assigned (leader == default)

errors.name: VaultFrozen
errors.msg: vault is frozen

errors.name: InvalidPrice
errors.msg: price bit pattern is not a valid encoding

errors.name: InvalidQuoteSlot
errors.msg: quote_slot is future-dated or backdated past MAX_BACKDATE

errors.name: ReferencePriceNotSet
errors.msg: set_liquidity_profile requires the vault's reference price to be set first

errors.name: LiquidityProfileSizeOverflow
errors.msg: liquidity profile size_bps sum exceeds 10_000 on one side

errors.name: OutsideDepositorsNotAllowed
errors.msg: leader has not enabled outside depositors on this vault

errors.name: OutsideDepositorsNotApproved
errors.msg: admin has not approved outside deposits on this vault

errors.name: SeedingRequiresLeader
errors.msg: first deposit to a vault must come from its leader

errors.name: SeedingRequiresBothLegs
errors.msg: first deposit to a vault must supply both base and quote legs

errors.name: SingleLegRequired
errors.msg: non-seeding deposit must size exactly one of base_in / quote_in

errors.name: BasketSlippage
errors.msg: derived basket exceeds caller's slippage bounds

errors.name: MinLeaderShareViolated
errors.msg: operation would violate the vault's min_leader_share floor

errors.name: InsufficientShares
errors.msg: requested shares exceed the caller's available stake

errors.name: InvalidAmountIn
errors.msg: swap amount_in must be greater than zero

errors.name: VaultDepositorMismatch
errors.msg: supplied VaultDepositor PDA does not match the (market, sector, owner) seeds

errors.name: MathOverflow
errors.msg: arithmetic overflow in basket / share math

errors.name: InvalidSwapSide
errors.msg: swap `side` argument is neither Buy nor Sell

errors.name: InvalidLimitPrice
errors.msg: limit_price sentinel is invalid for this swap side

errors.name: VaultAlreadyTombstoned
errors.msg: vault is already on the tombstone list

errors.name: TokenAccountNotEmpty
errors.msg: token account must be drained to zero before it can be closed

errors.name: MarketTreasuryNotClosed
errors.msg: market treasury must be closed before the market can be closed

errors.name: MarketHasDepositors
errors.msg: market still has outstanding VaultDepositor PDAs

errors.name: RegistryHasMarkets
errors.msg: registry still has live markets (market_count != 0)

errors.name: RegistryHasOtherAdmins
errors.msg: registry still has admins beyond the caller

errors.name: NotAMarketTreasury
errors.msg: supplied mint is not one of the market's base/quote legs

errors.name: TeardownDisabled
errors.msg: teardown instructions are disabled in this build (admin-teardown feature off)

errors.name: VaultTombstoned
errors.msg: vault has been closed and moved to the tombstone list

types.name: CloseVaultEvent
docs: Emitted by `close_vault` when a leader moves their vault from the
active DLL to the tombstone DLL. Matching stops; depositor flows
stay open until the vault drains. See the spec's **CloseVault**.

fields.name: active_count_after
docs: Active-DLL length after the move.

types.name: CreateVaultEvent
docs: Emitted by `create_vault`.

types.name: DepositEvent
docs: Emitted by `deposit` after share accounting + basis math.

types.name: FeeConfig
docs: A flat fee charged in `mint`, paid to the registry fee ATA. Mirrors
the `FeeConfig` in the architecture spec; reused per-market by the
`MarketHeader` once markets exist. Carrying `token_program`
alongside `mint` lets downstream fee-collection paths derive the
canonical ATA — `(authority, token_program, mint)` — without
guessing which token program owns the mint.

fields.name: mint
docs: Mint accepted for this fee.

fields.name: token_program
docs: Token program owning `mint` — SPL Token or Token-2022.

fields.name: atoms
docs: Amount in atoms of `mint`.

types.name: FillEvent
docs: Per-leg fill record. Bytemuck-serialized via `emit_cpi!` so the
inner-instruction data carries the canonical trade record at
`~1000 CU` + payload size per emit — the hot path can afford it.
Spec § **Events and emission → Granularity**: every leg is recorded,
no truncation. A sweep that exceeds one self-CPI's instruction-data
budget splits across multiple `emit_cpi!` calls.

fields.name: side
docs: `0` for ask-side (taker Buy), `1` for bid-side (taker Sell).

fields.name: _pad
docs: Padding so subsequent fields are aligned-1-friendly without
implicit struct padding the bytemuck check would reject.

fields.name: _pad2
docs: Padding to keep the next `u64` at an 8-byte boundary in the
fixed-size repr-C layout. `Price` is `u32`, so 4 bytes of pad
keep the struct stride well-defined.

types.name: FreezeVaultEvent
docs: Emitted by `freeze_vault` when an admin freezes a vault. The vault
stays on the active DLL (existing levels still match until expiry)
but can no longer be re-quoted. See the spec's **FreezeVault**.

types.name: MarketHeader
docs: Header of a market account. Followed by a slab tail of [`Vault`]
sectors. Per-market knobs are seeded from the registry at creation
and tunable downstream by admins.
All fields are alignment-1 — `Address`, `Pod*` wrappers, `[FeeConfig]`,
`u8` — so the header is padding-free and casts directly from the
account bytes.

fields.name: nonce
docs: Per-fill / per-quote monotonic counter.

fields.name: head
docs: Head of the active DLL: sector index or [`NULL_SECTOR`]. Walked
by the matching engine on every taker.

fields.name: tombstone_head
docs: Head of the tombstone DLL: sectors that have been `CloseVault`'d
but still hold outstanding shares. Not visited by matching.

fields.name: free_head
docs: Head of the free DLL: sectors available for reuse on `CreateVault`.
Singly linked via `next`; `prev` is ignored.

fields.name: active_count
docs: Active-DLL length. Bounded by `registry.max_vaults_per_market`.

fields.name: outstanding_vault_depositors
docs: Number of live `VaultDepositor` PDAs across every vault on this
market (active and tombstoned). Incremented when an outside
`Deposit` opens a fresh `VaultDepositor`, decremented when
`Withdraw` closes one on `shares == 0` and when
`force_withdraw_depositor` closes one. **Not** incremented on
top-off (existing `VaultDepositor`). `close_market` requires
this to be zero — the only on-chain witness that no orphan
depositor PDAs remain, since the program cannot iterate all
PDAs to verify by enumeration. See the architecture spec,
**Account lifecycle and rent reclamation**.

fields.name: fee_config
docs: Per-market create-vault fee: mint and amount. Seeded from
`Registry.default_fee_config` at market creation.

fields.name: taker_fee
docs: Taker fee rate, capped at ~6.55% (`Ppm16` max).

fields.name: default_min_leader_share
docs: Default min-leader-share for vaults opened on this market.
Stamped from `Registry.default_min_leader_share` at creation.

fields.name: base_mint
docs: Base leg mint.

fields.name: quote_mint
docs: Quote leg mint.

fields.name: base_treasury
docs: SPL / Token-2022 token account holding pooled base inventory.
ATA derived from `(market_pda, base_mint, base_token_program)`.

fields.name: quote_treasury
docs: Same as `base_treasury`, for the quote leg.

fields.name: bump
docs: Market PDA bump.

fields.name: base_treasury_bump
docs: Bump for the base treasury ATA derivation. Stored so transfers
out can sign with the market PDA without re-deriving.

fields.name: quote_treasury_bump
docs: Bump for the quote treasury ATA derivation.

types.name: Price
docs: A `u32` decimal floating-point comparison key.
```text
[5-bit biased exponent][27-bit normalized significand]
bits 31..27             bits 26..0
The significand is normalized to exactly 8 significant digits
(`10_000_000..=99_999_999`). The exponent is biased by 16 (unbiased
range `−16..=15`). The represented value is:
```text
value = significand × 10^(biased_exponent − 23)
= significand × 10^(unbiased_exponent − 7)
Two sentinel encodings: [`Price::ZERO`] (`0x0000_0000`, market sell)
and [`Price::INFINITY`] (`0xFFFF_FFFF`, market buy). All other valid
bit patterns represent regular prices.
**Integer order is price order.** With the exponent in the high bits
and the significand normalized to a fixed width, unsigned `u32`
comparison of two prices matches comparing the values they encode.
The encoding is canonical: one bit pattern per representable price.
**IDL note:** the Anchor `IdlType` derive is gated behind this crate's
`idl` feature (enabled by the on-chain program), so when `Price`
appears in an `#[account]` struct or instruction argument it emits as
raw `u32` bits. The off-chain / WASM builds leave `idl` off so the
crate stays solana-free.

types.name: RealizeEvent
docs: Emitted by `deposit` / `withdraw` whenever the implicit `Realize`
step mints new shares to the leader. Per spec, the hot path never
touches `Realize`, so swap does not emit this.

types.name: RegistryHeader
docs: Header of the global singleton `Registry`. Holds the protocol-wide
governance defaults stamped onto new markets; the admin allowlist
(the spec's `Set<Pubkey>`) is the slab tail — see [`Registry`].
All fields are alignment-1 (`Address`, `Pod*` wrappers, `u8`) so the
header is padding-free and casts directly from the account bytes.

fields.name: default_fee_config
docs: Default fee for the per-`CreateVault` charge, stamped into
`MarketHeader.fee_config` at market creation.

fields.name: market_count
docs: Number of live markets created against this registry. Incremented
by `create_market`, decremented by `close_market`. `close_registry`
requires this to be zero — the only on-chain witness that no
orphan markets remain, since the program cannot iterate all PDAs
to verify by enumeration. See the architecture spec, **Account
lifecycle and rent reclamation**.

fields.name: default_min_leader_share
docs: Default skin-in-the-game floor (ppm, [`Ppm32`]) stamped into markets.

fields.name: default_taker_fee
docs: Default taker fee (ppm, [`Ppm16`]) stamped into markets.

fields.name: max_vaults_per_market
docs: Default cap on vaults per market.

fields.name: bump
docs: Registry PDA bump.

types.name: VaultDepositorHeader
docs: Authoritative on-chain record of one depositor's position in one
vault — both the share claim and the cost basis the unrealized PnL
is measured against. All fields are alignment-1 so the struct casts
directly from the account bytes.

fields.name: market
docs: Market PDA this position is on. The vault is identified by
`(market, sector_idx)`; the PDA seeds include both so a sector
reuse across vault lifetimes derives a fresh address.

fields.name: sector_idx
docs: Sector index within the market's slab.

fields.name: owner
docs: Depositor wallet — bound by the PDA seeds, so the account is
non-transferable (there is no separate authority field to
reassign). See the spec's **Shares**.

fields.name: shares
docs: Pro-rata claim on the vault — the per-depositor term of
invariant I6 (`leader_shares + Σ VaultDepositor.shares ==
total_shares`).

fields.name: net_deposits
docs: Quote-denominated principal of the **remaining** position. Spec
formula (`Deposit` top-off): `Σ (quote_in + base_in × ref_now)`.
Reduced on `Withdraw` by the floored share-pro-rata slice.

fields.name: gross_deposited
docs: Lifetime contributions, monotonic — **never reduced on
withdraw**. Stable denominator for all-time return %.

fields.name: entry_ref_price
docs: Shares-weighted average reference price across deposits.

fields.name: entry_vps
docs: Shares-weighted average VPS (`L / total_shares`) across deposits,
Q32.32. Same encoding and practical bound as [`crate::Vault::hwm`].

fields.name: opened_at
docs: Slot of the first deposit. Captured once at PDA init.

fields.name: realized_pnl
docs: Signed quote-denominated PnL crystallized by past withdrawals.
Discarded when the account is closed at zero shares.

fields.name: realized_yield
docs: Signed yield (ex-FX) component of [`Self::realized_pnl`].

fields.name: realized_fx
docs: Signed FX component. Invariant:
`realized_yield + realized_fx == realized_pnl`.

fields.name: bump
docs: PDA bump.

fields.name: _reserved
docs: Padding so the on-chain size is a multiple of 8 and the
`#[account]` derive's discriminator + body length matches the
const-asserted total below.

types.name: WithdrawEvent
docs: Emitted by `withdraw` after share burn + basis crystallization.

fields.name: realized_pnl_delta
docs: Signed PnL delta crystallized on this withdrawal (outside path).
